zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jean Gaillard Gillard Gaylard Gaylord是Hugues de Beynac和Jeanne de Supplessan的儿子，于1330年出生在法国布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉哈特雷-德-布利塔尼。他和Inconnue De Chartres DeBretagne至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Gaillard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Philippe, Pierre, Andre, Andree, Ketly, Marcel, Mirlande, Monique.
French: nickname from gaillard ‘vigorous, strong’ (ultimately from Celtic galia ‘strength’). This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard, composed of the elements gail ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + hard ‘hard, strong’.

variants.name: Gailliard
meanings: French: variant of   Gaillard   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Gaylord
meanings: English (southern, of Norman origin): variant of Gaylard altered by folk etymology, from the Old French and Middle English personal name Gaillart, Gallard, Galliard, representing either the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard (composed of elements meaning ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + ‘hard, firm, resilient, strong’) or Old French gaillard, gaillart ‘vigorous, powerful, strong’. Alternatively, from Middle English gaillard, galiard ‘brave, strong, valiant’, also ‘lively, gay, gaily dressed’.
Altered form of Gaylard, itself an Anglicized form of French Gaillard , which was brought to England by the Huguenots.
In some cases also an Americanized form of French   Gaillard  .

nameMeanings.name: Gillard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Gillard, Jillard, with initial soft g (/dʒ/), a borrowing of Old French Gillard, a pet form of Gille (see Giles and 4 below). Alternatively, from the Middle English personal name Gillard which, with a hard g (/g/), would be from Old French Guilard, corresponding to the Norman form Willard .
English: sometimes perhaps an altered form of   Gillett  .
English: sometimes perhaps an altered form of Gerard (Middle English Girard) with r/l substitution.

variants.name: Giles
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from a medieval personal name, Middle English Giles or Gile, a borrowing from Old French Gil(l)e(s). This is from Latin Aegidius and this presumably from Greek aigidion ‘kid, young goat’ (alternatively, it could be a Late Latin formation from the Latin personal name Eggius + the suffix -idius). The personal name was widely used in France and the Low Countries, partly through veneration of Saint Gilles de Provence, supposedly a hermit of the 7th century near Arles; he was patron saint of cripples, hence the dedication of Saint Giles Cripplegate in London, though the personal name itself was less common in England than elsewhere in Europe. See also Gilles .
Irish: adopted as an Anglicized equivalent of Gaelic Ó Glaisne, a County Louth name based on glas ‘green, blue, gray’.
French: variant of   Gilles  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Izzard
meanings: English:

from the Middle English and Old French female personal name Iseld, Iseut, Isalt, Isaut, Isolde, Isoud, a French adaptation of Old Cornish Eselt ‘looked up to, admired’. Its currency in post-Conquest England derived from the legend of Tristan and Isolde, Cornish versions of which gained huge popularity in medieval Europe through romantic retellings by German, French, and Anglo-Norman writers.

from the Middle English female personal name Isot, itself a pet form of Iseld, Isold, names formerly but incorrectly identified as a hypothetical ancient Germanic Ishild(is), developing to Isheld, Ishaut, and Isholt. The surname Izzard shows post-medieval substitution of -ard in the final syllable of Middle English Iso(u)d (see 1 above) or Isot, influenced by the pattern of variation in names like Gillard , Gillett , and Gillott , or Garrard , Garrod , and Garrett , where in many instances the final -ard is original. Compare Ezzard , Isard , Isitt , Izard , and Izatt .

nameMeanings.name: Gaylard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English, Huguenot: (i) relationship name from the Old French personal name Gaillart (Continental Germanic * Gailhard ‘lofty’ + ‘hard’). (ii) nickname from Middle English gaillard, galiard ‘lively, brisk; gay, spirited’, Old French gaillard, gaillart.

variants.name: Gaillard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Philippe, Pierre, Andre, Andree, Ketly, Marcel, Mirlande, Monique.
French: nickname from gaillard ‘vigorous, strong’ (ultimately from Celtic galia ‘strength’). This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard, composed of the elements gail ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + hard ‘hard, strong’.

variants.name: Gaylord
meanings: English (southern, of Norman origin): variant of Gaylard altered by folk etymology, from the Old French and Middle English personal name Gaillart, Gallard, Galliard, representing either the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard (composed of elements meaning ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + ‘hard, firm, resilient, strong’) or Old French gaillard, gaillart ‘vigorous, powerful, strong’. Alternatively, from Middle English gaillard, galiard ‘brave, strong, valiant’, also ‘lively, gay, gaily dressed’.
Altered form of Gaylard, itself an Anglicized form of French Gaillard , which was brought to England by the Huguenots.
In some cases also an Americanized form of French   Gaillard  .

variants.name: Galliard
meanings: Norman English, French:  see  Gaylard  .

variants.name: Gallard
meanings: Norman English:  see  Gaylard  .

nameMeanings.name: Gaylord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern, of Norman origin): variant of Gaylard altered by folk etymology, from the Old French and Middle English personal name Gaillart, Gallard, Galliard, representing either the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard (composed of elements meaning ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + ‘hard, firm, resilient, strong’) or Old French gaillard, gaillart ‘vigorous, powerful, strong’. Alternatively, from Middle English gaillard, galiard ‘brave, strong, valiant’, also ‘lively, gay, gaily dressed’.
Altered form of Gaylard, itself an Anglicized form of French Gaillard , which was brought to England by the Huguenots.
In some cases also an Americanized form of French   Gaillard  .

variants.name: Gaillard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Philippe, Pierre, Andre, Andree, Ketly, Marcel, Mirlande, Monique.
French: nickname from gaillard ‘vigorous, strong’ (ultimately from Celtic galia ‘strength’). This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard, composed of the elements gail ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + hard ‘hard, strong’.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: Jeane
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jean .

en.lifeSummary: Jean Gaillard Gillard Gaylard Gaylord was born in 1330, in Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, France as the son of Hugues de Beynac and Jeanne de Supplessan. He had at least 1 son with Inconnue De Chartres DeBretagne.

fr.lifeSummary: Jean Gaillard Gillard Gaylard Gaylord est né en 1330, à Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, France, fils de Hugues de Beynac et Jeanne de Supplessan. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Inconnue De Chartres DeBretagne.

de.lifeSummary: Jean Gaillard Gillard Gaylard Gaylord wurde 1330 in Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Frankreich, als Sohn von Hugues de Beynac und Jeanne de Supplessan geboren. Er hatte mit Inconnue De Chartres DeBretagne mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Jean Gaillard Gillard Gaylard Gaylord è nato il nel 1330 nel Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia come figlio di Hugues de Beynac e Jeanne de Supplessan. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Inconnue De Chartres DeBretagne.

ja.Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, B: フランスブレタグンイレ-エト-ヴィレアインチャートレス-デ-ブレタグン
ja.lifeSummary: Jean Gaillard Gillard Gaylard Gaylordは1330年にフランスブレタグンイレ-エト-ヴィレアインチャートレス-デ-ブレタグンでHugues de BeynacとJeanne de Supplessanの息子として生まれた。彼はInconnue De Chartres DeBretagneとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean Gaillard Gillard Gaylard Gaylord(은)는 1330년 프랑스 브레타그니 이레-트-빌레이인 처트레스-데-브레타그니에서 Hugues de Beynac(와)과 Jeanne de Supplessan의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Inconnue De Chartres DeBretagne(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jean Gaillard Gillard Gaylard Gaylord nasceu em 1330, em Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, França filho de Hugues de Beynac e Jeanne de Supplessan. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Inconnue De Chartres DeBretagne.

ru.lifeSummary: Jean Gaillard Gillard Gaylard Gaylord родился в 1330 г. в местности чартрес-де-бретагн, илле-ет-вилеин, бретагн, Франция. Его родителей звали Hugues de Beynac и Jeanne de Supplessan. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Inconnue De Chartres DeBretagne.

es.lifeSummary: Jean Gaillard Gillard Gaylard Gaylord nació en 1330, en Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia como hijo de Hugues de Beynac y Jeanne de Supplessan. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Inconnue De Chartres DeBretagne.
